methods.description: Analysis of temperature and precipitation trends in the Marrakech area since 1901 using gridded climatology data to understand long-term climate change patterns.

steps.description: derived using the gridded climatology (Harris et al. 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain gridded climate data for Marrakech area since 1901
outputs: gridded climate data (temperature, precipitation) for Marrakech area since 1901

steps.description: The climate changes at Marrakech since 1901 are illustrated in Fig. 4...
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and illustrate trends in mean annual temperature, summer temperature, annual precipitation, and winter precipitation

methods.description: Calculation of theoretical modern melt totals for the Tazaghart Névé Permanent based on extrapolated temperature data from Marrakech Menara station and a degree-day factor to estimate potential snow/ice loss under modern conditions.

steps.description: ...based on extrapolations of observed monthly mean temperature data from the Marrakech Menara meteorological station.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain observed monthly mean temperature data from Marrakech Menara station
outputs: monthly mean temperature data for Marrakech Menara station

steps.description: based on extrapolations of observed monthly mean temperature data from the Marrakech Menara meteorological station.
steps.searchable_summary: extrapolate temperature data from Marrakech Menara station altitude to target altitude (3400 m)
inputs: monthly mean temperature data for Marrakech Menara station
outputs: extrapolated monthly mean temperature data at 3400 m

steps.description: At 3400 m the melt was estimated... when using degree-day factor of 4.1 mm °C −1 day −1 (Braithwaite 2008).
steps.searchable_summary: apply degree-day model with factor 4.1 mm °C −1 day −1 to calculate theoretical melt totals at 3400 m
inputs: extrapolated monthly mean temperature data at 3400 m
outputs: theoretical modern melt totals at 3400 m
